whatIs.title: What is a Temporary Email?
whatIs.subtitle: A free, anonymous inbox you can spin up in one click — perfect for the moments when you'd rather not hand over your real address.

points.p1Title: An inbox that doesn't follow you home
points.p1Text: A temporary email (also called disposable, throwaway, burner, or 10-minute mail) is a real, working email address that lives for a short time and then disappears — along with everything sent to it.
points.p2Text: Use it whenever a site asks for your email just to send a verification link, a download, or a coupon — without owning your inbox forever.
points.p3Text: No personal data, no account, no password. Spammers, data brokers and breach lists never see your real email.
points.p4Text: When the timer runs out, the address and every message in it are wiped — leaving zero trace behind.

useCases.title: Where Temp Mail saves the day
useCases.subtitle: Anywhere a site asks for your email just to "get started" — that's a perfect moment for a disposable address.

items.downloadsText: Grab that PDF, whitepaper or template without subscribing to a newsletter for life.
items.trialsText: Try a SaaS app or a streaming service without having to remember to cancel before charge day.
items.shoppingText: Buy from a store you'll never use again — no follow-up promo emails, no abandoned cart guilt.
items.paywallText: Get past 'sign up to continue reading' walls without giving up your inbox.
items.gameText: Play, comment, and post — without your real address ending up on a leaked database next year.
items.anonText: Sign up to surveys, beta tests, giveaways and contests with zero personal trail.
items.testText: Developers and QA: spin up fresh inboxes to test signup flows, password resets and emails.
items.breachText: If a site you used gets hacked, your real email never appears in any breach list.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you might want to know about disposable email and how MyTempMail keeps your inbox clean.

items.freeA: Yes — 100% free, forever. No signup, no credit card, no hidden 'pro' upsells. We don't sell your data because we don't collect any.
items.lastQ: How long does the email address last?
items.lastA: Each address lives for a limited time (shown next to your email as 'Expires in…'). Once it expires, the address and all messages are deleted automatically.
items.attachA: Yes. Most common attachment types — PDFs, images, documents — are received and shown in the message view, just like a regular inbox.
items.sendQ: Can I send emails from a temporary address?
items.sendA: No. MyTempMail is receive-only by design. This keeps the service fast, abuse-free, and impossible to use for spam.
items.regQ: Do I need to register or install anything?
items.regA: No. Open the page, click Generate, and you have a working inbox in under a second. There's nothing to install.
items.safeA: Yes. We don't ask for your name, phone or real email, and we don't track who owns which inbox. After expiry, everything is wiped — there's nothing left to leak.
items.customQ: Can I pick my own email name and domain?
items.customA: Absolutely. Click 'Custom email' to choose your own local part (the bit before the @) and pick a domain from the list.
items.acceptQ: Will websites accept a temp email?
items.acceptA: The vast majority do. Some banks, government sites and a few major platforms block disposable addresses — for everything else, it works perfectly.

faq.stillText: We try to keep things simple. If something isn't clear, we'd love to hear from you.
